summary: Learner loses track of what each variable stands for while writing an expression.
watchFor: Learner uses the same variable for different quantities or does not define the variable at all.
Learner writes a number-only answer even though the task still asks for an expression.
operatorNote: Check whether the learner can say what the variable represents before writing the symbolic relationship.

summary: Learner reads the visible symbols but not the structural roles inside the expression.
watchFor: Learner confuses coefficient with term count or constant term.
Learner treats terms of different degree or different variables as like terms.
operatorNote: Ask the learner to name one term, one coefficient, and one constant term separately before simplifying anything.

summary: Learner substitutes the value but loses sign, reciprocal, exponent, or grouping structure while evaluating.
watchFor: Learner substitutes correctly but loses a negative sign, reciprocal, or power during computation.
Learner treats a substituted expression as if terms can be freely reordered without preserving grouping.
operatorNote: Have the learner rewrite the full substituted expression once before simplifying it.

summary: Learner starts simplification but breaks the original structure while removing parentheses or collecting terms.
watchFor: Learner removes parentheses without tracking the outer sign or factor.
Learner combines unlike terms or changes the coefficient while collecting terms.
operatorNote: Pause the learner at the rewritten line and check whether only structurally matching terms are being combined.

summary: Learner can name the quantities but still writes the wrong relationship when turning a context into an expression.
watchFor: Learner chooses the right quantities but writes the wrong operation relationship.
Learner can build the first expression but cannot simplify it into a usable final form.
operatorNote: Keep the learner on the story relationship first and treat simplification as a second move.
